narration: Last time we built the difference quotient. Now let's see what happens as h goes to zero.
The secant line becomes the tangent line. That's the key.
This gives us the definition of the derivative. F prime of x equals the limit as h approaches zero of f of x plus h minus f of x, all over h.
Let's prove it works. Take f of x equals x squared.
Plug it into the definition. We get the limit as h goes to zero of x plus h squared minus x squared, over h.
Now we need to expand x plus h squared. That's x plus h times x plus h. FOIL it out: x times x, x times h, h times x, h times h. That simplifies to x squared plus 2xh plus h squared.
Substitute that back in. The x squareds cancel.
We're left with 2xh plus h squared over h. Factor out the h. Now the h's cancel.
Take the limit. As h goes to zero, we're left with just 2x.
Let's check. F prime of zero equals 2 times zero, which is zero. That's exactly what we saw — flat at the bottom. F prime of 2 equals 2 times 2, which is 4. The slope was 4. It works.
You just computed your first derivative using the definition.
